title: Static Site Generators Quiz
description: Test your knowledge of the essential concepts of pre-rendered web architectures and site generation tools.

questions.question: What is the primary role of a 'Static Site Generator' (SSG)?
options: To make the website load much faster by pre-rendering the entire site as simple, static HTML files during a 'build' process.
To automatically generate CSS animations for your site.
To provide a database that is only accessible during a specific time on the server.
To help users write HTML code using templates.

questions.question: Which of these is a major 'Pro' of using an SSG architected site compared to a traditional dynamic application?
options: It uses less data.
Extremely high-performance (speed) because pre-rendered HTML files can be served instantly from a CDN (Content Delivery Network).
It allows users to use the website without an internet connection.
It removes the need for any CSS rules.

questions.question: Which of these is a common 'SSG' tool mentioned in the article?
options: A regular calculator.
Next.js / Gatsby / Hugo.
A browser extension.
A simple 'ping' command.

questions.question: What is the primary drawback of using a statically pre-rendered site?
options: The browser cannot cache that CSS/JS for use on subsequent page visits.
The content remains 'stale' (stays the same) after the build until you trigger a new rebuild of the site.
Statically pre-rendered sites are not supported by any modern browser.
They are much more expensive to host.

questions.question: Which process handles automatically triggering a new site build whenever your content changes?
options: Manual Upload
Continuous Deployment
Static Refreshing
Browser Throttling
